default_title: Effective Smeared-Transfer Closure in a Clustered DK Sector
alternative_titles: Phase-Corridor-Controlled Texture Transition in a Clustered DK Branch
Reduction and One-Sided Closure of a Clustered DK Interaction Sector
objective: State the actual closed content of Phase III on the frozen clustered DK branch without enlarging the scientific scope.

sections.title: Title
sections.purpose: Set a restrained title that centers the smeared-transfer closure rather than braid discovery.
required_points: Use the default title unless editorial needs require one of the two restrained alternatives.

sections.title: Abstract
sections.purpose: State what failed, what survived, what closed, and what mechanism controls the local split.
required_points: Braid does not generalize beyond clustered seeds.
A valid two-state clustered-sector reduction survives.
Phase closure is one-sided and smeared-dominant.
Phase corridor is the primary control variable.

sections.title: Scope and branch discipline
sections.purpose: Freeze clustered-sector scope and prevent later inflation.
required_points: No family-wide intrinsic braid mechanism claim.
Texture-level braid is not promoted into a stable closed phase.

sections.title: Setup and frozen branch assumptions
sections.purpose: Summarize only the implementation constraints needed for interpretation.
required_points: Regular DK lattice and static Gaussian kernel class.
Graded-support skew surrogate where literal degree change is unavailable.
3x3 anchor lattice for the reduction stage.
Internal beta probe in the closure stage.
Bounded blockwise grade-fraction anchoring surrogate in the grade-transfer ablation.

sections.title: Non-generality of braid across families
sections.purpose: Present the decisive family-comparison negative result.
required_points: Interpretation: braid does not generalize beyond clustered seeds.

sections.title: Minimal effective reduction in the clustered sector
sections.purpose: State the surviving local effective validity without overturning non-generality.

sections.title: Effective phase diagram closure
sections.purpose: Show that the stable closed content is one-sided and smeared-dominant.

sections.title: Mechanism isolation
sections.purpose: State the clean mechanism sentence for the clustered sector.

sections.title: Final Phase III claim ledger
sections.purpose: Summarize the frozen positive core and negative boundaries in compact manuscript form.
required_points: universalization of clustered braid texture excluded

sections.title: Discussion
sections.purpose: Clarify the true content of the branch without enlarging its scope.
required_points: Phase closure lands on the smeared side.
Local braid texture remains descriptively useful but not structurally sovereign.

sections.title: Restricted correspondence to standard physics
sections.purpose: Clarify the limited, branch-bounded relevance of the frozen result to standard physics language without implying equivalence.
required_points: Restricted correspondence only at the level of effective modeling.
Local phase-controlled regime structure is compared only structurally, not identified with continuum theory.
Geometry overlap and 0 <-> 1 operator cross-coupling are bounded secondary supports rather than universal invariants.
No Maxwell, Dirac, gauge, spacetime, conserved-quantity, or observable-dictionary equivalence is licensed.

sections.title: Limits of interpretation
sections.purpose: State all remaining negative boundaries explicitly.
required_points: no claim beyond frozen operator class
reopening requires a structurally new branch

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.purpose: End with the frozen Phase III sentence in concise publishable form.
required_points: The surviving Phase III content is a finite smeared-transfer effective sector.
The local split is phase-corridor controlled.
Braid remains a clustered texture descriptor rather than a family-wide intrinsic mechanism.
